name: Lisas' Tomato Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel, seed, and coarsely chop the tomatoes (or substitute 2 cans of whole, undrained tomatoes, 20 oz each).
2. Set aside.
3. In a large saucepan, cook the onion in butter until tender.
4. Add the chopped tomatoes, bay leaf, brown sugar, basil, cloves, salt, and pepper to the saucepan.
5. Bring to a boil and cover. Simmer, stirring occasionally, for 25 minutes or until the tomatoes are thoroughly cooked (reduce cooking time to 15 minutes if using canned tomatoes).
6. Remove the bay leaf and cloves, and let the soup slightly cool.
7. Blend half of the mixture in a food processor or blender, then strain it through a fine-mesh strainer into a clean bowl.
8. Repeat steps 6-7 with the remaining mixture.
9. Return both blended mixtures to the saucepan.
10. Add the milk and half and half to the soup, stirring until well combined.
11. Heat the soup over medium-low heat until it is hot throughout (do not boil).
12. Serve sprinkled with fresh chives.
